trends.name: AI Cost Competition & DeepSeek Impact
trends.summary: China's DeepSeek technology triggered a broad AI market rout, raising questions about cost efficiency and competitive threats to U.S. AI giants. Despite fears, analysts argue established U.S. AI titans remain defensible, but sentiment has shifted toward valuation concerns in the sector.

trends.name: Geopolitical Risk & Safe Haven Flight
trends.summary: Iran conflict escalation is driving capital toward safe havens (Swiss franc, Japanese yen, gold) and raising inflation concerns, potentially complicating Fed interest rate decisions. Berkshire Hathaway's fortress positioning reflects investor anxiety about broader geopolitical destabilization.

trends.name: Interest Rates & Inflation Uncertainty
trends.summary: Conflicting economic signals—jobless claims at multi-month lows and consumer credit surging, but productivity slowing and unit labor costs accelerating—are clouding the Fed's rate outlook. Geopolitical tension adds inflation uncertainty on top of labor cost pressures.

trends.name: Oil & Energy Market Volatility
trends.summary: Trump administration efforts to control $100 oil prices are failing as geopolitical risks persist. Shale producers may increase output (benefiting midstream like EMLP), but tariff uncertainty and Iran conflict keep energy markets volatile and unpredictable.

trends.name: Equity Market Weakness & Support Breakdown
trends.summary: S&P 500 has broken a major support level with analyst warnings of another 10% decline likely. Broad market weakness spans multiple sectors including AI, retail, and financials, suggesting systemic concerns beyond single-stock issues.

trends.name: Tariff Impact & Consumer Pricing
trends.summary: Retailers are not passing tariff costs back to consumers through refunds, squeezing margins and explaining retail sector weakness. Coffee prices hit records amid U.S.-Colombia tariff disputes, raising inflation concerns for consumers and compressed profitability for companies.

trends.name: EV & Autonomous Vehicle Sector Shift
trends.summary: Rivian's $45,000 EV launch represents market shift toward affordable EVs, while Uber-Zoox partnership proves autonomous vehicles are an opportunity rather than existential threat. Sector positioning is changing as cost competition intensifies.

trends.name: AI & Technology M&A Activity
trends.summary: Netflix's up-to-$600M acquisition of Ben Affleck's AI startup signals tech giants' aggressive move into AI capability acquisition. This reflects intense competition and potential bubble dynamics in AI-adjacent M&A activity.

trends.name: Software & SaaS Margin Challenges
trends.summary: SailPoint shows strong ARR momentum but stalled margins, while Adobe beats earnings yet stock falls—indicating investor focus has shifted from growth to profitability. SaaS sector facing margin compression and efficiency scrutiny.

trends.name: Specialty Finance & Consumer Credit Risk
trends.summary: Atlanticus, Pagaya, and America's Car-Mart earnings suggest consumer finance stress despite strong credit growth headline. Underlying credit quality concerns and valuation risks exist in specialty finance as rate environment remains uncertain.

trends.name: Retail & Consumer Discretionary Weakness
trends.summary: Ulta Beauty cites geopolitical conflicts impacting consumer spending, signaling retail discretionary sector facing headwinds. Beyond's Buy Buy Baby acquisition and broader retail M&A suggest sector consolidation amid consumer caution.

trends.name: Niche Sector Opportunities & Restructuring
trends.summary: Coal (Ramaco), water utilities, building products (QXO-Beacon bid), and energy infrastructure plays offer specific opportunities for patient investors, though many carry execution and timeline risks. Sector-specific M&A activity signals consolidation trends.
